title: FUGUEの実装を確認し、Kernelとの差分を埋めて。ただしFUGUE/kernel両方の既存のシステムを損壊しないように慎重に実装をマルチエージェントで進めて。
summary_text: 1. **Critical — `attach-context` can overwrite completion lifecycle markers (`last_event`, `summary`) after a run is completed, creating lifecycle ambiguity.** `attach-context` writes `update attach_context_reference ...`, which unconditionally rewrites `last_event` and bounded summary fields in compact artifact; this can replace `run_completed` as the latest lifecycle signal. [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:663](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:663) [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:667](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:667) [scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:601](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:601) [scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:602](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:602) [docs/kernel/interfaces/contracts.md:26](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/interfaces/contracts.md:26) 2. **High — Event contract drift: `attach_context_reference` is used as compact event, but not part of frozen event contract.** Current contract enumerates event set; new attach event is outside that set and not reflected in contract docs. [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:667](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:667) [docs/kernel/interfaces/contracts.md:15](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/interfaces/contracts.md:15) 3. **High — Idempotent/no-op behavior is missing for repeated attach; same reference still mutates compact artifact.** There is no equality check against existing `context_reference`; repeated attach rewrites `updated_at`/`last_event` instead of no-op. [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:640](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:640) [scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:358](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:358) [scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:606](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:606) 4. **Medium — Compact schema drift: `context_reference` is now persisted, but Kernel schema contract is not updated.** Runtime writes additive field `context_reference`, while schema contract still defines compact required/known structure without this field. [scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:600](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-compact-artifact.sh:600) [docs/kernel/interfaces/schema-v1.md:98](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/interfaces/schema-v1.md:98) [docs/kernel/interfaces/track-compat.md:93](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/docs/kernel/interfaces/track-compat.md:93) 5. **Medium — Prompt/handoff boundary is weak: unbounded label/path are injected directly into operator prompt and handoff summary.** `attach-context` accepts free-form label and stores it; downstream prompt/handoff surfaces print it verbatim, so long/multiline labels can expand context or alter prompt shape. [scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:643](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/codex-kernel-guard.sh:643) [scripts/lib/kernel-codex-thread.sh:69](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/lib/kernel-codex-thread.sh:69) [scripts/local/kernel-handoff-summary.sh:152](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/kernel-handoff-summary.sh:152) 6. **Medium — Provider-agnostic handoff boundary drift: attached reference format is FUGUE/Claude-specific and path portability is host-local.** Generated reference embeds `main_orchestrator`, `assist_orchestrator`, `claude_session_count`, and local `run_dir` paths, then gets attached into Kernel compact state used for continuation/handoff. [scripts/local/fugue-context-reference.sh:86](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/local/fugue-context-reference.sh:86) [scripts/local/fugue-context-reference.sh...
